question: When did Nebuchadnezzar beseige Jerusalem (Dan 1:1)?
options: In Jehoiakim’s third year
In Zedekiah’s fifth year

In Gedaliah’s first year

Shalmaneser
Nebuchadnezzar
Sennacherib

question: Who delivered Jehoiakim into Nebuchadnezzar’s hand (Dan 1:2)?

Belshazzar
The nations
The Lord

question: Jehoiakim was described as the king of _________ (Dan 1:1)
options: Israel
Judah
Jerusalem

question: Ashpenaz was to bring Israelites from what two groups to Babylon (Dan 1:3)?

Royal family and nobility
Officials and craftsmen

question: What did the king assign the Israelite captives daily (Dan 1:5)?
options: Learning and exercise
The worship of his gods
Food and wine

question: What new name did Daniel receive in Babylon (Dan 1:7)?
options: Nebuzaradan
Arioch
Belteshaazr

question: Mishael received the new name of ________ from the chief official (Dan 1:7)

question: Azariah received the new name of ________ from the chief official (Dan 1:7)
options: Meshach
Abednego
Ebed-melech
Shadrach

question: What did Daniel resolve not to do (Dan 1:8)?
options: Deny his God by worshipping the gods of Babylon
Deny his identity by wearing the clothes of Babylon
Defile himself with the royal food and wine
Pollute himself by sleeping in the king’s palace

question: What did God do to the chief official (Dan 1:9)?
options: Caused him to show favor and sympathy to Daniel
Caused him to have leperousy
Gave him a heart that was kind and compassionate
Protected him from the king

question: What test did Daniel propose to the guard who was over him (Dan 1:12)?
options: Give him water and vegetables for 10 days
Give him wine and vegetables for a month
Give him bread and water for 15 days
Give him water and lambs for 7 days

question: What did God give the four young men in Babylon (Dan 1:17)?
options: Wisdom and discipline
Knowledge and understanding
Insight and discernment
Brilliance and eloquence

question: What special gift did Daniel have (Dan 1:18)?
options: Understanding dreams and visions
Interpreting the times and the seasons
Understanding the ways of the Babylonians

question: How did the king determine Daniel’s wisdom and understanding (Dan 1:20)?
options: He asked them to interpret the literature of Babylon
He gave them a puzzle
He had his servants test them for three days
He questioned them

question: Daniel and his friends were said to be better than the _________ (Dan 1:20)
options: Wisemen and sages
The scribes and advisors
The officials and satraps
The magicians and enchanters

question: What happened to Nebuchadnezzar in his the second year of his reign (Dan 2:1)?
options: He captured Jerusalem
He tested Daniel’s wisdom and understanding
He had dreams and his mind was troubled
He made a tower in Babylon for his gods

question: Why did Nebuchadnezzar summon the magicians and enchanters (Dan 2:2)?
options: To interpret his vision
To interpret the letter from the Medes and Persians
To tell him how to end the war
To tell him what he had dreamed

question: What did the astrologers request of King Nebuchadnezzar (Dan 2:4)?
options: For three days to figure out the dream
For him to enter the temple to receive the interpretation
For him to tell them the dream
For him to summon all Babylon

question: What orders was Arioch, the commander of the king’s guard, given (Dan 2:14)?
options: To round up all the wise men of Babylon
To put to death all the wise men of Babylon
To destroy the homes of all the astrologers of Babylon

question: How did God reveal to Daniel the king’s dream (Dan 2:19)?
options: He struck him down with the bright light like the sun
He spoke to him through the Urim and Thummim

He had a vision during the night

question: Why did Daniel praise the God of his fathers (Dan 2:23)?
options: He made know to them the dream of the king
He rules over heaven and earth
He moved the heart of Nebuchadnezzar like a leaf

question: What was Daniel’s other name, i.e. his Babylonian name (Dan 2:26)?

question: Who did Daniel tell Nebuchadnezzar reveals mysteries (Dan 2:28)?

The God of all the earth

question: While Nebuchadnezzar was on his bed to what did his mind turn (Dan 2:29)?

The burning of Jerusalem

question: Where was Nebuchadnezzar when his dream and visions came to him (Dan 2:28)?
options: On the wall of Babylon
On the throne in his palace

question: In Nebuchadnezzar’s unknown dream what stood before him (Dan 2:31)?

A goat pushing from the west

Bronze

question: What would the iron kingdom do (Dan 2:40)?

Ride forth on iron chariots

question: What would be characteristic of the fourth kingdom of iron and clay (Dan 2:42)?
options: It would be the strongest kingdom ever
It would be a divided kingdom
It would be strong but worthless

question: What would be characteristic of the fourth kingdom of iron and clay (Dan 2:42)?
options: It would be the strongest kingdom ever
It would be strong but worthless
It would be a partly strong and partly brittle

question: What did the stone represent (Dan 2:44)?
options: A kingdom God would set up that would last forever
The work of God overcoming all other kingdoms

options: Four men walking around in the fire unbound and unharmed

question: What did Nebuchadnezzar ask Daniel to do in regard to the tree dream (Dan 4:9)?

It had no roots

question: What was unique about the tree Nebuchadnezzar had in his dream (Dan 4:11)?
options: It was visible to the ends of the earth
It was on fire and was smoking to the sky

question: All of the following were benefits of the dream tree EXCEPT (Dan 4:12)

options: On his throne
While being carried to the temple

His kingdom was going to fall

So that they might know the Most High is sovereign over the kingdoms

options: It was burned
It was used as to sit on

question: Who will become even stronger than the king of the South (Dan 11:5)?
